name: Lydia's Mexican Casserole
recipeIngredient: 2 cups tomato salsa (I used commercially prepared; fresh stuff in the deli section)
1 red chili pepper or 1 jalapeno chili, chopped
1 27-ounce can whole green chilies (like Ortega brand)
1 tablespoon dry, oil-free salad dressing mix
Freshly chopped cilantro for garnish (or parsley)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Using a nonstick baking dish or spraying lightly with cooking spray, line the casserole with 6 tortillas.
3. Mix together onion, tomatoes, salsa, 1 teaspoon ground cumin, chopped chili and garlic. Pour about 3/4 of this mixture into the casserole.
4. Lay the whole green chili peppers flat over this, then the mashed tofu. Cover with remaining tortillas.
5. Add remaining tomato mixture. Mix yogurt with the dry salad dressing mix and pour over the top, sprinkle a little ground cumin over this.
6. Bake for about 35 to 45 minutes.
7. Garnish with cilantro or parsley.
Variation:
Substitute some cooked beans for the tofu, try soy yogurt for vegan option.
Notes:
Make it as hot as you like, just add more chilies.
Makes: 8 servings (more like 4 - 6 at my house)
